title: Inkle-woven bookmark set
subtitle: Six narrow inkle bands in contrasting cotton, finished as bookmarks with knotted fringe
excerpt: Weave a set of six narrow bookmarks on an inkle loom using cotton thread in contrasting colour pairs. Each bookmark takes about 20 minutes to weave, and the finished set makes a practical gift. No reed is needed; the inkle loom uses a combination of heddle strings and open warp threads to create the shed.
sourceNotes: Synthesised from traditional inkle-loom weaving practice. Inkle-woven bookmarks are a standard beginner inkle project documented in British and American guild teaching.

glossaryTerms.definition: A simple loom with a series of fixed and adjustable pegs and a set of heddle strings. It weaves narrow bands by alternating between two sheds: one formed by lifting the heddle strings, one by pressing them down.
A weave in which the weft is completely hidden by closely-set warp threads. In inkle weaving, the pattern shows entirely in the warp colour arrangement, not in the weft.
The finished woven edge running lengthways along the band. On an inkle band, keeping neat selvedges requires consistent weft tension at each turn.

content.text: : the pattern comes entirely from the warp colour sequence and the weft is invisible. A bookmark set needs clean
and even tension to look well-made; the inkle loom's continuous warp makes tension easy to manage.

items.name: Cotton perle or 10/2 cotton in two contrasting colours per bookmark

content.text: Wind a warp of 24 to 30 threads (12 to 15 per colour in an alternating sequence) around the pegs of the inkle loom. Every other warp thread passes through a heddle string on the front peg; the alternating threads sit freely above. This gives the two sheds. Set the warp on the loom at a length of about 40 cm for a finished bookmark of 30 cm.

Open the first shed by lifting the heddle strings. Pass the weft through and beat gently with a finger or a flat stick. Open the second shed by pressing the heddle strings down and pass the weft back through. Repeat, maintaining even tension at each selvedge turn. The alternating colour warp will produce a simple stripe pattern running lengthways along the band.
Step 3: Add fringe and cut off'

Cut the band off the loom, leaving at least 5 cm of warp at each end. Separate the warp ends into small groups and tie each group into a simple overhand knot close to the last woven row. Trim the fringe to a consistent length of about 3 cm. Repeat for each bookmark in the set.

items.problem: The selvedges draw in and the band becomes narrower in the middle than at the ends
items.cause: The weft tension is too tight at each turn, pulling the outer warp threads inward
items.fix: Leave a very slight arc or loop of weft in each shed before beating, so the weft is not under tension when it packs down.

items.problem: The two colour stripes are unequal in width
items.cause: The alternating warp sequence was not maintained consistently, giving more threads to one colour than the other
items.fix: Count the threads per colour before weaving. A correctly wound inkle warp has exactly equal numbers per colour; if not, rewind the warp.
